activities: Market analysis: demand for agile web shops remains high as enterprises accelerate digital transformation, while boutique agencies win by coupling AI-first development with deep TypeScript/Next.js expertise (sources: LogRocket’s 2026 web dev trends, Mordor Intelligence 2026 market report).

Role mapping: identified seven essential agents (ResearchLead, DesignLead, ProvisioningLead, ValidationLead, DeliveryCoordinator, SecurityAdvisor, ObservabilityEngineer) that cover intelligence, architecture, deployment, QA, stakeholder cadence, security, and telemetry.

Competitive positioning: pitch the Business OS as an isolated, ready-to-run AI/agent-enabled web practice with embedded guardrails—clients trade faster delivery plus audited safety instead of piecing together disparate tooling.
competitive_positioning: Frame the OS as a nimble AI-augmented development studio where human leads supervise autonomous agents for design iteration, infrastructure spinning, and defense-in-depth validation.
Explain that the Business OS is turnkey—workspace_path, state_dir, config_path, and agent_roster are all pre-defined, mirroring the agency’s delivery pipeline without side effects on other projects.
Highlight embedded guardrails (compliance, performance, accessibility) to reassure procurement teams that innovation does not compromise security.

design_plan.summary: Translate research into a repeatable set of org structure, artifacts, and responsibilities so the Web Development Agency OS can scale via clear handoffs and files that every agent owns.

agent_org_chart.flow: ResearchLead collects intelligence and hands requirements to DesignLead and ProvisioningLead; DesignLead refines architecture while ProvisioningLead orchestrates the workspace; ValidationLead, SecurityAdvisor, and ObservabilityEngineer enforce readiness; DeliveryCoordinator translates status for stakeholders.

role_definitions.ResearchLead: Translates market intelligence into requirements, prioritizes agent tasks, and chairs the research-to-design handoff.
role_definitions.DesignLead: Owns architecture, defines agent interactions, and creates templates that ProvisioningLead consumes to manifest the OS.
role_definitions.ProvisioningLead: Executes workspace creation, maps workspace_path/state_dir/config_path, and runs deployment scripts for the agent roster.
role_definitions.ValidationLead: Operates QA/compliance matrices, reviews telemetry, and automates guardrails before granting readiness.
role_definitions.DeliveryCoordinator: Keeps stakeholders informed, drives demos, logs experiments, and maintains the status board.
role_definitions.SecurityAdvisor: Approves risk posture, owns access controls, and signs off on the ValidationLead’s readiness declaration.
role_definitions.ObservabilityEngineer: Designs telemetry, alerting, and dashboards so the provisioning pipeline is observable and auditable.

council_structure.reporting_lines: ResearchLead chairs the council, feeds requirements into Design/Provisioning, ValidationLead presents QA status, SecurityAdvisor gates release, ObservabilityEngineer informs telemetry health, DeliveryCoordinator supplements stakeholder updates.
council_structure.cadence: Daily standups plus thrice-weekly readiness syncs and ad-hoc check-ins for anomalies.

sequence: 1. ResearchLead captures template goals, agent roster, and acceptance criteria (Day 0, 2h).
2. DesignLead finalizes OS blueprint, including workspace_path/state_dir/config_path mapping and agent orchestration specs (Day 0, 3h).
3. ProvisioningLead creates state_dir, clones template, applies configs, and deploys agent roster with dedicated compute/storage (Day 1 morning).
4. ValidationLead automates QA, compliance scans, guardrail verifications, and signs off on readiness (Day 1 afternoon).
5. ObservabilityEngineer wires dashboards/alerting; DeliveryCoordinator prepares stakeholder demo and readiness digest (Day 2).
6. Council convenes for SecurityAdvisor signoff, risk review, and readiness communication (Day 2 afternoon).
7. Operations handoff with runbooks, documentation, and final artifact archival (Day 3).

resource_requirements.compute: Two dedicated compute nodes (provisioning/orchestration + AI design agents) with GPU acceleration for generative tooling.
resource_requirements.storage: Persistent workspace storage >=1TB for assets plus redundant state_dir/config_path volumes to support immutability and rollbacks.
resource_requirements.network: Segmented network with VPN peering, HTTP/HTTPS egress controls, and service endpoints for AI/observability APIs.
resource_requirements.agent_capacity: Seven active agent slots for Research, Design, Provisioning, Validation, Delivery, Security, and Observability roles, plus two standby slots for burst tasks.

provisioning_plan.estimated_time_to_operational: 3 business days (7 work cycles) from kickoff; readiness gated by ValidationLead and SecurityAdvisor signoff with council approval.
